preview: {"paper_id": "10.1002_srin.202200207", "title": "Optimization of Process Conditions for Ultralightweight Steel with More Than 13 wt% of Al and 5 wt% of Cr", "abstract_summary": "Development of ultralightweight Fe-30Mn-13.2Al-1.6C-5Cr steel with >13 wt% Al achieving 20% density reduction. Optimization of hot rolling (soaking at 1100-1200°C) and heat treatment (homogenization at 1050°C followed by air cooling vs water quenching) to suppress brittle phase formation. Successful fabrication at 1100°C soaking vs cracking at higher temperatures. Water-quenched specimen exhibits excellent tensile properties (1048 MPa, 38% elongation) with uniform austenite and fine κ-carbide, while air-cooled specimen shows brittle behavior due to DO3 phase at grain boundaries.", "research_objective": "To manufacture ultralightweight Fe-Mn-Al-C steel containing >13 wt% Al (20% density reduction) by suppressing formation of brittle coarse κ-carbide, ferrite, and ordered phases at grain boundaries through Cr addition and optimization of hot rolling and heat treatment conditions.", "material_systems": ["Fe-30Mn-13.2Al-1.6C-5Cr", "ultralightweight steel", "austenitic steel", "κ-carbide", "DO3 ordered phase", "BCC phase"], "main_process_variables": {"hot_rolling": {"soaking_temperature_range": "1100-1200°C", "soaking_time": "2h", "successful_temperature": "1100°C", "failed_temperatures": ["1150°C", "1200°C"], "rolling_temperature": ">1000°C"}, "heat_treatment": {"homogenization_temperature": "1050°C", "holding_time": "2h", "cooling_methods": ["air cooling", "water quenching"]}}, "sample_aliases": ["1100°C soaked specimen", "1150°C soaked specimen", "1200°C soaked specimen", "air-cooled specimen", "water-quenched specimen", "hot-rolled plate"], "expected_information_axis": ["composition-density relationships", "soaking temperature effects on grain size and DO3 phase formation", "cooling rate effects on phase precipitation and mechanical properties", "intergranular cracking mechanisms", "tensile property optimization", "Cr addition effects on κ-carbide suppression"], "key_sections": ["Introduction", "Results and Discussion", "Conclusions", "Experimental Section"]}

{
  "paper_id": "10.1002_srin.202200207",
  "title": "Optimization of Process Conditions for Ultralightweight Steel with More Than 13 wt% of Al and 5 wt% of Cr",
  "abstract_summary": "An ultralightweight Fe–30Mn–13.2Al–1.6C–5Cr steel with 20% density reduction was developed. Hot rolling was successful after soaking at 1100°C, while soaking at 1150 or 1200°C caused intergranular cracking from coarse grains and ferrite/DO3 at grain boundaries. Homogenization at 1050°C followed by water quenching produced excellent tensile properties; air cooling led to brittle DO3 and poor ductility.",
  "research_objective": "Develop an ultralightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C steel containing >13 wt% Al and 5 wt% Cr by suppressing brittle phases (coarse κ-carbide, ferrite, ordered DO3) through optimized hot rolling and heat treatment, achieving good tensile properties and 20% density reduction.",
  "material_systems": [
    "Fe–30Mn–13.2Al–1.6C–5Cr (wt%) ultralightweight steel"
  ],
  "main_process_variables": [
    "Soaking temperature before hot rolling: 1100, 1150, 1200°C for 2 h",
    "Hot rolling above 1000°C, air cooling",
    "Homogenization at 1050°C for 2 h",
    "Cooling after homogenization: air cooling vs. water quenching"
  ],
  "sample_aliases": {
    "hot_rolled": [
      "1100°C soaked plate",
      "1150°C soaked plate",
      "1200°C soaked plate"
    ],
    "heat_treated": [
      "air-cooled specimen",
      "water-quenched specimen"
    ]
  },
  "expected_information_axis": [
    "Microstructural evolution (austenite grain size, fraction and morphology of DO3 phase, κ-carbide distribution)",
    "Phase identification (XRD, TEM, EBSD phase maps)",
    "Tensile mechanical properties (strength, ductility, fracture behavior)",
    "Process-property correlations (soaking temperature, cooling rate vs. cracking, tensile performance)"
  ],
  "key_sections": [
    "Introduction",
    "Results and Discussion",
    "Conclusions",
    "Experimental Section"
  ]
}

{
  "paper_id": "10.1002_srin.202200207",
  "title": "Optimization of Process Conditions for Ultralightweight Steel with More Than 13 wt% of Al and 5 wt% of Cr",
  "abstract_summary": "An ultralightweight Fe–30Mn–13.2Al–1.6C–5Cr steel with >13 wt% Al, reducing density by 20%, was developed and successfully fabricated by optimizing hot rolling and heat treatment. Soaking at 1100 °C before hot rolling avoided intergranular cracking, while higher soaking temperatures (1150, 1200 °C) caused coarse grains and brittle ordered DO3 phase at grain boundaries leading to cracks. After homogenization at 1050 °C for 2 h, water quenching produced a uniform microstructure (austenite, fine κ-carbide, minimal DO3) and excellent tensile properties (1048 MPa UTS, 38% elongation), whereas air cooling resulted in grain boundary DO3 and premature brittle fracture.",
  "research_objective": "To develop an ultralightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C steel containing more than 13 wt% Al by suppressing brittle phases (coarse κ-carbide, ferrite, ordered DO3) through Cr addition and optimization of hot rolling and heat treatment conditions to achieve successful fabrication and good tensile properties.",
  "material_systems": [
    "Fe–30Mn–13.2Al–1.6C–5Cr (wt%) ultralightweight steel"
  ],
  "main_process_variables": [
    "Hot rolling soaking temperature (1100, 1150, 1200 °C)",
    "Soaking time (2 h)",
    "Homogenization temperature (1050 °C)",
    "Homogenization time (2 h)",
    "Cooling method after homogenization (air cooling, water quenching)"
  ],
  "sample_aliases": [
    "1100 °C soaked plate",
    "1150 °C soaked plate",
    "1200 °C soaked plate",
    "air-cooled specimen",
    "water-quenched specimen"
  ],
  "expected_information_axis": [
    "Soaking temperature → microstructure (grain size, DO3 phase fraction and morphology) and hot-rollability",
    "Cooling method → presence of DO3 phase and tensile properties",
    "Phase identification (austenite, κ-carbide, ordered DO3)",
    "Tensile strength and total elongation"
  ],
  "key_sections": [
    "1. Introduction",
    "2. Results and Discussion",
    "3. Conclusions",
    "4. Experimental Section"
  ]
}

{
  "paper_id": "10.1002_srin.202200207",
  "title": "Optimization of Process Conditions for Ultralightweight Steel with More Than 13 wt% of Al and 5 wt% of Cr",
  "abstract_summary": "An ultralightweight Fe–30Mn–13.2Al–1.6C–5Cr steel with >13 wt% Al reduces density by 20% but is brittle. Hot rolling was optimized with soaking at 1100–1200°C for 2 h; only soaking at 1100°C enabled successful rolling without intergranular cracking, attributed to finer austenite grains and less/brittle DO3 phase at grain boundaries. Homogenization at 1050°C for 2 h followed by water quenching gave excellent tensile properties (1048 MPa, 38% elongation) due to uniform austenite, fine κ-carbide, and minimal DO3, while air cooling led to brittleness from DO3 at grain boundaries.",
  "research_objective": "To fabricate ultralightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C steel with >13 wt% Al (20% density reduction) by suppressing brittle κ-carbide, ferrite, and ordered phases through Cr addition and optimization of hot rolling and heat treatment conditions.",
